Comprehensive search of setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py, and all seed data shows this value does not exist anywhere in the task. The phrase only appears in task.json metadata field itself. Task description mentions department options: Sales, Engineering, HR, Support - but never Human Resources Specialists.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic employee survey called '2024 Employee Voice' with 15 pre-seeded responses preserved in the database. Initial survey has 3 questions: tenure (numeric), satisfaction (5-point scale), and comments (long text). The task requires adding a new 'dept' (department) radio question with options Sales, Engineering, HR, and Support. One specific response contains the comment 'The flexible hours are the best part of working here' which serves as a data integrity check. All data is generated locally via Python JSON-RPC API calls and direct SQL inserts, with no external data sources. The 'occupation_context' metadata value 'Human Resources Specialists' does not appear in any task setup, execution, or verification logic.
